label: A2 — Elementary
blurb: 5 partes, 25 preguntas: imágenes, completar, opción múltiple y emparejar.

audios.title: Part 1 · Questions 1–5
audios.intro: For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C). You will hear five short conversations. Use the arrow ▶ or the numbers below to move to the next conversation.
scripts: Question one. Girl: Did you buy the camera you wanted, Leo? Boy: I saved up for the camera, but then I saw a guitar in the music shop and bought that instead. Girl: Wow, can you play it? Boy: Not yet!
Question two. Mum: Your guitar lesson is at four o'clock, isn't it? Boy: It was, but my teacher changed it to half past four. Mum: Then we can have a snack first.
Question three. Girl: Is the school concert in the park this year? Boy: No, it might rain, so it's in the big café next to the school. Girl: Nice and warm — good.
Question four. Woman: Will the music festival be OK tomorrow? Man: The forecast says sunny all day — not like last year's rain! Woman: Finally some luck.
Question five. Dad: Shall I drive you to your piano lesson? Girl: No thanks, Dad — it's a lovely day, so I'll go by bike. Dad: Take care on the road.

questions.q: What time is the boy's guitar lesson now?
Where is the school concert this year?
What will the weather be like at the festival tomorrow?
How will the girl go to her piano lesson?

audios.title: Part 2 · Questions 6–10
audios.intro: You will hear a teacher telling students about music lessons at school. For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one word or a number or a date or a time.
scripts: Listen to a teacher talking about school music lessons. Teacher: Good news, everyone — school music lessons start again in September. This year you can learn the drums as well as the piano and the violin. Lessons are on Mondays, in the school hall. Each lesson costs four pounds. If you want to join, write your name on the list outside the music room before Friday. And remember — the school can lend you an instrument for free.

questions.label: Write your name on the list before: ____

audios.title: Part 3 · Questions 11–15
audios.intro: You will hear Ruby talking to her friend Max about their band practice. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Ruby: Max, can we practise at your house on Thursday? Max: My mum works at home on Thursdays — the noise drives her crazy. What about Wednesday? Ruby: Fine, Wednesday then. I'll bring my keyboard. Max: Isn't it really heavy? Ruby: My brother can carry it in his car. Max: Great. Should we ask Elena to sing with us? Ruby: I already did — she's got a cold, so she'll join us next week. Max: OK. Oh, and can you print the song words? My printer is broken. Ruby: Sure, no problem.

questions.q: When will Ruby and Max practise?
Why can't they practise on Thursday?
o: Max's mum works at home that day

questions.q: How will the keyboard get to Max's house?
Why can't Elena come this week?

audios.title: Part 4 · Questions 16–20
audios.intro: You will hear a boy called Ethan talking about learning the violin. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Ethan: I started violin lessons two years ago because my best friend played, and I didn't want to be left out. He stopped after six months, but I kept going — funny, isn't it? At first I practised only ten minutes a day, but now I do half an hour before dinner every evening. My little sister covers her ears when I play, but my grandad loves listening. He always said I should play in the school orchestra, and last week the music teacher asked me to join! We'll play our first concert in December.

questions.q: Why did Ethan start learning the violin?
o: He heard it at a concert

questions.q: How long does Ethan practise now?
Who loves listening to Ethan play?
Who asked Ethan to join the orchestra?

audios.title: Part 5 · Questions 21–25
audios.intro: You will hear Clara talking about her music club. What does each person play? For each question, choose the correct answer.
scripts: Clara: Everyone in our music club plays something different. Ben plays the drums — his neighbours aren't very happy about it! Sofia plays the piano; she's been learning since she was five. Hugo plays the trumpet, really loudly. Kate plays the flute — it sounds like a little bird singing. And Noah plays the guitar and writes his own songs.
